examples.title: TCP Slow Start Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how TCP Slow Start exponentially increases congestion window
examples.explanation: In Slow Start, cwnd increases by 1 MSS for each ACK received, effectively doubling cwnd every RTT. This continues until cwnd reaches ssthresh, then transitions to Congestion Avoidance.

examples.title: Fast Retransmit and Fast Recovery
examples.description: Shows TCP Reno's handling of packet loss via duplicate ACKs
examples.explanation: When 3 duplicate ACKs are received, TCP Reno performs Fast Retransmit and enters Fast Recovery. It reduces cwnd to ssthresh/2 + 3 and continues sending new data during recovery, unlike Tahoe which goes back to Slow Start.

examples.title: RED Algorithm Implementation
examples.description: Shows Random Early Detection drop probability calculation
examples.code:
// RED Algorithm Parameters
minthresh = 5 // Minimum threshold
maxthresh = 15 // Maximum threshold 
maxP = 0.1 // Maximum drop probability
qw = 0.002 // Weight for avg queue calculation

avgq = 0 // Average queue size

FUNCTION calculateDropProbability(packet):
 // Update average queue size
 avgq = (1 - qw) * avgq + qw * currentQueueSize
 
 IF avgq < minthresh:
 // Below minimum: no drops
 return 0
 ELSE IF avgq >= minthresh AND avgq < maxthresh:
 // Between thresholds: linear drop probability
 p = maxP * (avgq - minthresh) / (maxthresh - minthresh)
 return p
 ELSE:
 // Above maximum: drop all
 return 1

FUNCTION onPacketArrival(packet):
 dropProbability = calculateDropProbability()
 
 IF random() < dropProbability:
 drop(packet)
 ELSE:
 enqueue(packet)
examples.explanation: RED calculates the average queue size and drops packets probabilistically based on where the average falls between minthresh and maxthresh. This provides early congestion notification to senders before queues overflow.

examples.title: Token Bucket Algorithm
examples.description: Traffic shaping algorithm allowing bursts up to bucket capacity
examples.code:
// Token Bucket Parameters
bucketCapacity = b // Maximum tokens bucket can hold
tokenRate = r // Tokens added per second
tokens = b // Initial tokens (full bucket)

FUNCTION addTokens(deltaTime):
 tokensAdded = tokenRate * deltaTime
 tokens = min(bucketCapacity, tokens + tokensAdded)

FUNCTION sendPacket(packetSize):
 tokensNeeded = packetSize // Typically in bytes/MSS
 
 IF tokens >= tokensNeeded:
 tokens = tokens - tokensNeeded
 return true // Packet sent
 ELSE:
 return false // Must wait or drop

// Traffic Policing Example
FUNCTION processPacket(packet):
 addTokens(timeSinceLastPacket)
 
 IF NOT sendPacket(packet.size):
 // Option 1: Wait for tokens (shaping)
 // Option 2: Drop packet (policing)
 drop(packet)
examples.explanation: Token Bucket allows bursts up to the bucket capacity (b tokens) while enforcing an average rate (r tokens/second). Tokens are consumed when packets are sent, and new tokens are added at a constant rate.
